content.problemStatement:
Given a positive integer NNN, construct an array AAA containing NNN distinct\textbf{distinct}distinct elements such that the sum of any two elements in the array (not necessarily different) is not present in the array.

That is, there have to be no\textbf{no}no such i,j,ki, j, ki,j,k such that


1≤i,j,k≤N1 \le i, j, k \le N1≤i,j,k≤N
Ai+Aj=AkA_i + A_j = A_kAi​+Aj​=Ak​.
The elements of the array AAA should be in the range [1,105][1, 10^5][1,105]. It is guaranteed that such an array always exists under given constraints.


Input Format

First line of the input contains TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains a single positive integer NNN, the size of the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, print NNN space-separated integers in a single line, describing the contents of the array AAA.
constraints: All output numbers must be between 1 and 100000 inclusive.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 1000).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output N distinct integers separated by a single space on one line. The integers must be in the range [1, 100000] and must satisfy the condition that the sum of any two (possibly equal) elements is not present in the array.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=1 we output the first odd number: 1.\nFor N=2 we output 1 and 3; sums are 2,4,6 none of which are in the array.\nFor N=3 we output 1,3,5; all pairwise sums are even (2,4,6,8,10) and thus not present.

solution.approach:
Observe that the sum of two odd numbers is always even. Therefore, if we output only odd numbers, no sum of any two (including the same element) can be another odd number, i.e., cannot belong to the array. The simplest construction is to output the first N odd numbers: 1, 3, 5, ..., 2·N‑1. All numbers are distinct, lie in [1, 1999] for the maximal N=1000, well below the allowed limit 100000, and satisfy the required property.
Algorithm for each test case:
1. Read N.
2. For i from 0 to N‑1 output (2*i + 1).
The algorithm runs in O(N) time per test case and uses O(1) additional memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T·N) overall.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        line = ' '.join(str(2*i+1) for i in range(N))
        out_lines.append(line)
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
